title: andinia peruviana
intro: Andinia peruviana, also known as the Andean mint, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountains in Peru. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Andinia peruviana has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in various herbal preparations, such as salves and creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
